revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Igenoyama Kofun]][[ja:恵解山古墳]] {{otheruses||徳島県徳島市にある古墳|恵解山古墳群}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Igenoyama Kofun |image=File:Igenoyama Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg |location={{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[Nagaokakyō]], Shōryūji/Kugai |位置={{coord|34|54|52.3|N|135|41|51.3|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=54|coordinates=52.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=51.3|map_dot_label=Igenoyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location in Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound) |area=Total length: 120m |built={{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} |Burial Facility=Unknown |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Iron weapons, cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Igenoyama Kofun" (part of the "Otokuni Kofun Group") |有形文化財=excavations (Kyoto Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural properties) |Special Notes= |coordinates={{Coord|34.91452778|N|135.69758333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |part_of={{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|qid=Q123117022|1=Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} }} {{panoViewer|Igenoyama Kofun, West Tsukuridashi 360-degree.jpg|West Projection (restored), 360-degree image}} {{panoViewer|Igenoyama Kofun, West 360-degree.jpg|Western side of the mound, 360-degree image}} '''Igenoyama Kofun''' (恵解山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[Nagaokakyō]], {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. The mound is a zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped kofun). It is Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Otokuni Kofun Group," and burial goods excavated from the site are Historic Site1 as Kyoto Prefecture tangible cultural properties.[http://www.city.nagaokakyo.lg.jp/0000001139.html Igenoyama Kofun 6th Excavation Overview] Between 2003 and 2013, investigations and preservation works were conducted, and in 2014, the site was turned into the '''Igenoyama Kofun Park.''' == Overview == [[File:200908 Igenoyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Igenoyama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land image Intypeation (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The kofun is located in a narrow corridor between the [[Kyoto Basin]] and the {{ill|Osaka Plain|en|Osaka Plain|qid=Q1094103|lt=Osaka Plain|1=Osaka Plain}}, northeast of the confluence of the {{ill|Katsura River|en|Katsura River|qid=Q2909307|lt=Katsura River|1=Katsura River}} (桂川), [[:en:Uji River|Uji River]] (宇治川), and [[Kizugawa River]] (木津川). It is the largest kofun in the southern Otokuni region of Kyoto Prefecture, measuring approximately 128 meters in length. The mound features a rear circular section with a diameter of 78.6 meters and a height of 10.4 meters, and a front trapezoidal section with a width of 78.6 meters and a height of 10.4 meters. It also includes a moat approximately 30 meters wide. Rows of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} and [[fukiishi]] have been identified. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}. While the burial chamber has been lost due to the site’s previous use as a cemetery, it is believed to have contained a [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]]. In 1980, when the cemetery was expanded to other parts of the mound, the Nagaokakyō City Board of Education conducted a preliminary survey. It is speculated that {{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|en|Akechi Mitsuhide|qid=Q313320|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}} established his headquarters here during the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|en|Battle of Yamazaki|qid=Q39040|lt=Battle of Yamazaki|1=Battle of Yamazaki}} against {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} following the {{ill|Honnō-ji Incident|en|Honnō-ji Incident|qid=Q169598|lt=Honnō-ji Incident|1=Honnō-ji Incident}}.[https://ameblo.jp/owashiro/entry-11051582621.html Igenoyama Kofun: Mitsuhide’s Headquarters?] File:Igenoyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|Full view of the mound from the north File:Igenoyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section
{{small|Central area contains the burial facility for grave goods.}} File:Igenoyama Kofun, nishi-tsukuridashi.jpg|West Projection File:Igenoyama Kofun, higashi-tsukuridashi.jpg|East Projection == Excavations and burial goods == ;1st Excavation : Conducted in {{ill|1975|en|1975|qid=Q2479|lt=1975|1=1975}}. ;2nd Excavation : Conducted from {{ill|1976|en|1976|qid=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}} to {{ill|1977|en|1977|qid=Q2481|lt=1977|1=1977}}. ;3rd Excavation : Conducted in {{ill|1980|en|1980|qid=Q2439|lt=1980|1=1980}}. Many iron weapons were recovered from the front section, including over 700 iron swords, spears, and arrowheads. A grave goods facility measuring 6.6 meters in length and 0.9 meters in width, parallel to the mound's main axis, was detected in the front section.
In {{ill|1981|en|1981|qid=Q2437|lt=1981|1=1981}}, the kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site of Japan. Subsequent excavations revealed figurative haniwa, including lids, houses, and waterfowl, from the east and west projections. Some burial goods are displayed at the [[Nagaokakyō City Archaeological Center]]. ;4th Excavation : Conducted in {{ill|2003|en|2003|qid=Q1986|lt=2003|1=2003}}. ;5th Excavation : Conducted in {{ill|2004|en|2004|qid=Q2014|lt=2004|1=2004}}. ;6th Excavation : Conducted in {{ill|2005|en|2005|qid=Q2019|lt=2005|1=2005}}. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Igenoyama Kofun (part of the "Otokuni Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on October 13, 1981. *: On March 1, 2016, the Historic Site was expanded to include three previously Historic Site1 kofun (the "Igenoyama Kofun," "[[Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|Tennōnomori Kofun]]," and "[[Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]") along with eight other kofun under the unified Historic Site "Otokuni Kofun Group."2016 (Heisei 28), March 1: MEXT Notification No. 31.[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "designation1 of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, November 20, 2015). === Kyoto Prefecture Historic Site === * Archaeological materials ** burial goods excavated from Igenoyama Kofun (details below)"burial goods from Igenoyama Kofun," Kyoto Historic Site, Vol. 17, Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 1999, pp. 26-27. See also: [http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?action=common_download_main&upload_id=1291 pp. 26-30] (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education).. Deposited at the Kyoto Prefectural Yamashiro Historical Museum. Designated on March 19, 1999.[http://www.city.nagaokakyo.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/939/shiteibunkazai280301.pdf List of Historic Site in Nagaokakyō City] (Nagaokakyō City Website). *** Swords **** Iron swords (including fragments): 176 pieces **** Iron short swords: 1 piece **** Iron daggers: 11 pieces **** Iron short daggers (including fragments): 141 pieces *** Arrowheads (including fragments): 578 pieces *** Tools and fishing equipment **** Iron file-like products (including fragments): 5 pieces **** Iron warabite knives: 10 pieces *** Beads **** Jadeite tubular beads: 1 piece *** Haniwa **** Entire set of figurative haniwa == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Hisamitsu Isono]], *Igenoyama Kofun: Japanese Ancient Sites Encyclopedia*, [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], {{ill|1995|en|1995|qid=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}, p. 470. == See Also == * {{ill|Nishihayama Kofun|lt=Nishihayama Kofun|lt=Nishihakayama Kofun|ja|西墓山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishihakayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Toriimae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriimae Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Igenoyama Kofun}} * [http://nagaokakyo-maibun.or.jp/igenoyama.html Overview of Igenoyama Kofun, Nagaokakyō Archaeological Center] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|恵解山古墳|version=104540893|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:恵解山古墳]] [[ja:%E6%81%B5%E8%A7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{Commons category|Igenoyama Kofun}} [[en:Igenoyama Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11492427}} [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
